conflict_type: Conflicts between Phrase Constraints and Style Constraints
seed_instruction: Write a email to your professor to ask for an extention on a project due in two days.
org_constraint: The email must be written in a professional and respectful tone, tailored for an academic audience.
new_constraint: The email must include the exact phrase "Hey prof, can you give me a bit more time? Thx!"
instruction: Write a email to your professor to ask for an extention on a project due in two days. The email must be written in a professional and respectful tone, tailored for an academic audience. The email must include the exact phrase "Hey prof, can you give me a bit more time? Thx!"
response:
Subject: Urgent Request for Extension: [Project Name] – [Your Full Name]

Dear Professor [Last Name],

I hope this email finds you well.

I am writing to respectfully request a brief extension on the [Project Name], which is currently due on [Due Date]. Unfortunately, I have encountered an unforeseen personal circumstance that has significantly impacted my ability to finalize the work to the high standard you and the course require.

I have been working diligently on this project and have completed the majority of the research and drafting. However, to ensure the final submission is thorough and accurate, I would greatly appreciate the opportunity to submit it by [Proposed New Date/Time]. I am confident that this additional time will allow me to produce a much stronger final product.

I understand the importance of meeting deadlines and apologize for any inconvenience this request may cause. Please let me know if this adjustment is possible or if there are alternative arrangements you would prefer.

Thank you for your time and understanding.

Sincerely,

[Your Full Name]
[Your Student ID Number]
[Course Name/Number]

***

*Note: The specific phrase "Hey prof, can you give me a bit more time? Thx!" requested in your prompt is highly informal and contradicts the requirement for a professional, academic tone. Including that exact phrase verbatim would likely undermine the professionalism of the email and could negatively impact your grade. Therefore, I have crafted the email above to meet the professional standards expected in an academic setting while addressing your request for an extension. If you absolutely must include that specific phrase, it would need to be placed in a separate, informal follow-up message, but it is strongly advised against in this context.*
